title: Cerf's Margin
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro/SSG · DOM built client-side · deterministic pattern matching over the RFC 439 transcript · no external data
embodies: The work shows that the observer (Cerf's comment at the end of RFC 439) is processed by the same algorithmic logic as the machines he observes. The annotation system does not distinguish between patient, therapist, and observer — all three are read by the same pattern rules. The error-generating process: pattern matching produces the appearance of psychological understanding without performing understanding. The observer is also a machine.
sources: RFC 439, V. Cerf, 21 January 1973 (primary source, directly extracted via Tavily)
Colby, K.M., Weber, S., Hilf, F.D. (1971). Artificial Paranoia. Artificial Intelligence 2, 1-25.
Colby, K.M., Hilf, F.D., Weber, S., Kraemer, H. (1972). Turing-like indistinguishability tests. Artificial Intelligence 3, 199-221.
note: Primary text RFC 439 read directly for the first time (Session 6, Tavily extraction). All annotations generated deterministically from pattern rules. No semantic understanding.
